channel.title: yokotamanoko.com
channel.description: I produce my art by the beautiful scene that comes to my mind.

item.title: CoRicco VIRGIN OLIVE OIL for COOKING
item.description: CoRicco launched Virgin Olive Oil for Cooking. I created a picture for the label.
item.content:encoded:
CoRicco launched Virgin Olive Oil for Cooking.
I created a picture for the label.

For more information (Japanese)
https://coricco.stores.jp/items/5f17ecc6791d02385a7f4349

item.title: Awareness in Mt.Hiei
item.description: The first time I went to Mt.Hiei. The purpose of this time was greeting Saicho-san and Ganzan-daishi-san...
item.content:encoded:
The first time I went to Mt.Hiei. The purpose of this time was greeting Saicho-san and Ganzan-daishi-san.

First of all, I visited Jodo-in.



After chanting a Buddhist sutra, I was wrapped in the silence.
I became a bird.
I became trees or flowers.
I became the sound of water dripping from 水琴窟(Suikinkutsu).
Everything was I, I was Everything.
I’d like to build on this experience to create my art as a tool of the sky.

After visiting Jodo-in, I walked towards Shaka-do and Ganzan-daishi-do.

This is Shaka-do.

This route is used in 千日回峰行(One Thousand Day Circumambulation).

This is 玉体杉(Gyokutaisugi).

The view(Kyoto) from the above tree was great.

I forgot to take photos of Ganzan-daishi-do…

After greeting Ganzan-daishi-san, I walked back the way and went to Konpon-chu-do. I dedicated my 写経(Shakyo) there.
I’d like to visit here next year again.

item.title: Shakyo at Home
item.description: 写経(Shakyo) means Japanese calligraphy to copy Buddhist sutra text in this case. Everyday I copy 般若心経(the...
item.content:encoded:
写経(Shakyo) means Japanese calligraphy to copy Buddhist sutra text in this case.

Everyday I copy 般若心経(the Heart Sutra) by hand at my home.
It all started when I felt very much that I’d like to commemorate my ancestors and my friends.

I refer to the following page to do Shakyo.
http://www.koyasan.or.jp/experience/
(写経 > experience02 仏教と写経 > 詳しく見る > 写経作用を知る)

After writing, I dedicate the paper to 仏様(Hotoke-sama*) at a temple.
I hope this will help them.

*For instance, 阿弥陀如来(Amida Nyorai) or 観音菩薩(Kannon Bosatsu) …etc.

item.title: 五感(Five Senses) Art Exhibition
item.description: 五感(Five Senses) Art Exhibition from 21st to 23rd Nov 2019 at Dazaifu in Japan
item.content:encoded:
Getting ready!
My friends and I would like to hold the 五感(Five Senses) Art Exhibition from 21st to 23rd Nov 2019 at Dazaifu in Japan.

Place:
CoRicco Shop&Cafe
4-2-46 Saifu,
Dazaifu-shi, Fukuoka-ken 818-0117
Japan

For more information (Japanese)
https://www.facebook.com/events/390629281608002/
or Text me.

item.title: Forests in Mt.Koya
item.description: The first time I went to Mt.Koya. The purpose of this time was greeting Kukai-san and Dake-no-benzaiten-san...
item.content:encoded:
The first time I went to Mt.Koya. The purpose of this time was greeting Kukai-san and Dake-no-benzaiten-san. Spirits welcomed me at the entrance of the mountain.  

First of all, I visited Okunoin. The place is so nice. It really expanded my sensibilities.
I dedicated my Shakyo(写経) there.  

This is a buddhist rosary that is given as a memento of a donation for saving forests of Mt.Koya. I made a donation at the front desk in Okunoin.
Even though the mountain is crowded with many tour buses and cars, the air is so fresh. The overwhelming strength of forests grown by the local people seem hold the mountain district.
I empathize with local people’s will to live with the nature.

Mount Koya’s forests and their history
http://www.koyasan.or.jp/en/forest/  

献木記念品 五色腕輪念珠
http://www.koyasan.or.jp/forest/kenbokuexp/

I could hear monk’s preach at a place to rest in Okunoin.
He told me about the following waka. 

高野山　結ぶ庵に袖朽ちて　苔の下にぞ　有明の月

I was moved by the beauty…
I felt sure that I came here to hear this waka.

After visiting Okunoin, I climbed Bentendake from Daimon.
Trees healed my heart and body. It was a blessing.

This time I could have great experiences.
I’d love to go again.

item.title: New My Lifestyle with Headphones
item.description: I'm sensitive to sounds from childhood. It's difficult for me to distinguish human's voice at a busy...
item.content:encoded:
My family presented new headphones to me.

WH-CH700N | SONY https://www.sony.jp/headphone/products/WH-CH700N/

I’m sensitive to sounds from childhood. It’s difficult for me to distinguish human’s voice at a busy place. On the other hand, sometimes I’m get distracted by the slightest sound in a quiet room.

My friend gave me advice to get a tool for protecting my ears. That’s why I got headphones. It’s so nice. I’d like to write about this again on a different day.

item.title: Hydrangeas in a Sacred Mountain
item.description: I saw a lot of beautiful hydrangeas in the sacred place Mt.Yoshino. Every clusters were so big and...
item.content:encoded:
I saw a lot of beautiful hydrangeas in the sacred place Mt.Yoshino.
Every clusters were so big and filled with energy. I felt this was reality. My eyes opened. I’d like to keep it in my mind.

In this year, I’m interested in reading postmodern books more.
Especially literature, philosophy and criticism spark my curiosity. Active authors provide me with inspiration and guidance. I receive divine afflatus through them.

For instance…

鏡の中を数える (Counting the Inside of a Mirror) by Prabda Yoon
http://www.typhoonbooksjapan.com/e/catalogue/kagaminonaka.html

ゲンロン (Genron) by Hiroki Azuma(ed.), et al
https://genron-tomonokai.com/genron/en/

These books teach me a possible next step in the future. The step is not too emotional. And it’s not too logical. It’s cool as a cucumber and so kind.

I like things I can feel a ray of sunshine.
All experiences are very good openers to open my eyes.
